dataset.description: The dataset contains four tables: batter_player_stats (batting statistics for individual players), bowler_player_stats (bowling statistics for individual players), detailed_player_data (per-match player performance data), and match_summary (match-level details including toss results, match outcome, player of the match, and venue information).

columns.description: The name of the batter (player) in the dataset.
columns.name: player_name

columns.description: The role of the player, set to 'Batter' for all entries in this table.
columns.name: role

columns.description: Total career runs scored by the batter.
columns.name: total_runs

columns.description: Average runs per hundred balls scored by the batter.
columns.name: strike_rate

columns.description: Total balls faced during batting by the batter.
columns.name: total_balls_faced

columns.description: Total wickets taken by the player as a bowler (if applicable).
columns.name: total_wickets_taken

columns.description: Total runs conceded by the player as a bowler (if applicable).
columns.name: total_runs_conceded

columns.description: Total overs bowled by the player.
columns.name: total_overs_bowled

columns.description: Total matches played by the batter.
columns.name: total_matches_played

columns.description: Number of matches played by the player primarily as a batter.
columns.name: matches_played_as_batter

columns.description: Number of matches played by the player primarily as a bowler.
columns.name: matches_played_as_bowler

columns.description: Number of matches won by the player's team while the player was in the match.
columns.name: matches_won

columns.description: Number of matches lost by the player's team while the player was in the match.
columns.name: matches_lost

columns.description: Count of player-of-the-match awards received by the player.
columns.name: player_of_match_awards

columns.description: Team (country or league) the player represents.
columns.name: team

columns.description: Batting average calculated as total runs divided by the number of times out.

desc: The dataset contains four tables: batter_player_stats (batting statistics for individual players), bowler_player_stats (bowling statistics for individual players), detailed_player_data (per-match player performance data), and match_summary (match-level details including toss results, match outcome, player of the match, and venue information).
goal: Analyze which individual player performances contribute most to match victories, focusing on the best players' batting and bowling statistics and their impact on the match outcome.
name: Visual Analytics System cricket_goal3_1742641993

plan.path: The user begins by browsing View 1 at the [Overview] level to observe the overall distribution of players' fantasy points and match outcomes. Next, they select a range of fantasy points on the x-axis in View 1, causing View 2 to filter for associated player statistics at the [Exploration] level. Then, the user toggles between batting and bowling metrics in View 2 and drills into individual matches in View 3 at the [Detail] level to focus on extreme performances for runs scored or wickets taken. Finally, highlighted match IDs from View 3 are passed to View 4 at the [Insight] level to uncover broader impacts of player contributions on match results and team victories, visualized through aggregated metrics like boundaries and player-of-match effects.

effect.description: Filter range of fantasy points selected on the x-axis to narrow down player data in exploration views.

visualization.description: Scatter plot showcasing all players from the dataset. The x-axis represents fantasy points achieved, and the y-axis denotes match outcomes. Points are color-coded by outcome and sized by runs scored.

views.components: Add dropdown menus to toggle between runs and wickets metrics for sorting and filtering.

effect.description: Switch visibility between batting and bowling statistics.

visualization.description: Bar chart displaying combined batting and bowling metrics for players. Bar segments are color-coded to denote match outcomes.

effect.description: Highlight match IDs for selected extreme performances to pass as context to insight views.

visualization.description: Tick chart showcasing individual matches with extreme performances for runs scored or wickets taken.

views.components: Add hover functionality to display details of player-of-match contributing to the victory.

effect.description: Show contextual details for the player of the match and match impact metrics.

visualization.description: Bar chart ranking match winners by total boundaries scored and identifying the impact of player-of-match awards.

data.reason: To identify which metrics influence match outcomes and analyze patterns of consistent performance related to winning matches.
data.requirement: Aggregate player performance metrics (e.g., runs scored, wickets taken, player_of_match_awards, and fantasy_points) at the individual player level over all matches.

data.reason: To analyze the impact of a player's contributions specifically in matches won versus lost.
data.requirement: Filter the dataset for matches where the player was part of the winning team and compare to matches when the player was not on the winning side.

data.reason: To combine batting and bowling data for players who contribute as all-rounders and provide a holistic view of their impact.
data.requirement: Join data from `detailed_player_data`, `batter_player_stats`, and `bowler_player_stats` tables to provide comprehensive details on each player's match performance.

data.reason: To identify players with a history of consistently high performance and correlate these to match outcomes.
data.requirement: Aggregate data to calculate career averages for specific statistics such as batting strike rate, economy rate, and average runs scored per match.

data.reason: To identify standout individual performances that directly lead to match victories.
data.requirement: Highlight players with extreme performances (e.g., highest runs in a match or most wickets in a match) and correlate these to matches won.

data.reason: To help identify if certain players or teams perform better against specific opposition or in specific conditions.
data.requirement: Provide a breakdown of performance metrics by opposition team and venue.

interaction.reason: To enable personalized exploration of player performance data and match-specific impact.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to filter visualizations based on specific players, teams, opposition, or venues using dropdown menus or multi-select filters.

interaction.reason: To provide detailed contextual insights without cluttering the visualizations.
interaction.requirement: Enable hover-over functionality for data points in scatter plots, line charts, and heat maps to display detailed statistics such as runs scored, wickets taken, and match outcome for specific data points.

interaction.reason: To facilitate temporal analysis of performance trends and detect periods of peak performance.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to zoom in and out on line charts to focus on specific time periods or trends for selected players.

interaction.reason: To connect macro-level patterns to micro-level statistics and provide a seamless exploration experience.
interaction.requirement: Enable users to click on a player or match in the visualizations to drill down into a detailed view of their statistics for that match.

interaction.reason: To enable comparative analysis and highlight players or conditions with the highest contributions.
interaction.requirement: Allow users to sort bar charts (e.g., by total runs, wickets, awards) and heat maps (e.g., by total performance metrics for opposition teams or venues).

interaction.reason: To ensure users can customize views to their specific areas of interest.
interaction.requirement: Include toggles to switch between metrics (e.g., from strike rate to economy rate) in applicable visualizations for flexible analysis.

interaction.reason: To facilitate identification of outlier performances that are often the most impactful.
interaction.requirement: Provide the ability for users to query and visualize only extreme performances (e.g., top 5% for runs, wickets, or fantasy points) across visualizations.

visualization.reason: To easily identify players who are consistently impactful in winning matches.
visualization.requirement: Use a bar chart to display the top 10 players by total `player_of_match_awards` and compare the distribution between matches won and lost.

visualization.reason: To analyze the correlation between individual performances and match outcomes effectively.
visualization.requirement: Use a scatter plot to visualize the relationship between significant metrics like runs scored, wickets taken, and match outcomes, with color encoding for win/loss.

visualization.reason: To identify patterns or hotspots of strong performances under specific conditions.
visualization.requirement: Use a heat map to compare aggregated performance (e.g., runs scored, wickets taken) across different venues and opposition teams.

visualization.reason: To provide insight into the consistency of top players within key statistics.
visualization.requirement: Use a box plot to visualize the distribution of `strike_rate` and `economy_rate` for the top-performing players.

visualization.reason: To analyze trends in individual player performance and detect periods of peak impact.
visualization.requirement: Use a line chart to track the performance over time of specific players, focusing on their impact metrics (e.g., runs, wickets, fantasy points).

visualization.reason: To analyze all-round performance and its impact on matches won or lost.
visualization.requirement: Use a bar chart with stacked segments to represent combined batting and bowling contributions, color-coded by match outcome.

visualization.reason: To easily spot outliers and connect them to match results.
visualization.requirement: Use a tick chart to highlight standout performances (e.g., highest runs in a match or most wickets in a match) and correlate these to match outcomes.

visualization.reason: To provide an overview of the role-specific contributions to match victories.
visualization.requirement: Use a pie chart to represent the proportion of matches won by players in specific roles (e.g., batter, bowler, all-rounder).

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW intermediate_view_1 AS SELECT detailed_player_data.player, detailed_player_data.fantasy_points, detailed_player_data.match_outcome, batter_player_stats.total_runs, bowler_player_stats.total_wickets_taken FROM detailed_player_data JOIN batter_player_stats ON detailed_player_data.player = batter_player_stats.player_name JOIN bowler_player_stats ON detailed_player_data.player = bowler_player_stats.player_name;
sqls.name: intermediate_view_1

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW intermediate_view_2 AS SELECT detailed_player_data.match_id, detailed_player_data.runs, detailed_player_data.wickets, detailed_player_data.match_outcome FROM detailed_player_data;
sqls.name: intermediate_view_2

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW intermediate_view_3 AS SELECT match_summary.match_id, match_summary.outcome_winner, match_summary.player_of_match, match_summary.total_fours, match_summary.total_sixes FROM match_summary;
sqls.name: intermediate_view_3

tasks.method: Aggregate player-level metrics using a bar chart to rank the top players by total `player_of_match_awards`. Use stacked segments to differentiate between wins and losses.
tasks.objective: Identify which players consistently perform at the highest level and contribute most to match victories.

tasks.method: Filter the dataset for matches where the player's team won and compare these metrics to matches where their team lost, using a scatter plot with color encoding for win/loss.
tasks.objective: Analyze the correlation between individual player performance metrics and match outcomes.

tasks.method: Join tables to create a comprehensive dataset for each player. Use a bar chart with stacked segments showing combined batting and bowling metrics, color-coded by match outcome.
tasks.objective: Analyze all-rounder contributions and their overall impact on match results.

tasks.method: Calculate career averages for players and visualize using box plots to compare distributions of `strike_rate` and `economy_rate` for top-performing players.
tasks.objective: Identify players with consistent performance over their careers and correlate these metrics with match outcomes.

tasks.method: Retrieve and highlight extreme performances using a tick chart to showcase matches with the highest runs scored or wickets taken and their impact on match outcomes.
tasks.objective: Spot standout individual performances that directly lead to match victories.

tasks.method: Create a heat map to visualize aggregated player performance (e.g., runs scored, wickets taken) across different venues and opposition teams.
tasks.objective: Identify patterns of strong performances under specific conditions.

tasks.method: Use a line chart to track player performance over time, with hover-over functionality to display contextual details for specific matches.
tasks.objective: Analyze temporal trends in individual player performance and detect periods of peak impact.

tasks.method: Use a pie chart to visualize the proportion of matches won by players categorized by their role (batter, bowler, all-rounder).
tasks.objective: Provide an overview of role-specific contributions to match victories.
tasks.type: computing derived values | aggregating data | sorting

tasks.method: Allow users to sort and filter bar charts by metrics such as total runs or wickets to compare contributions across teams using dropdown menus.
tasks.objective: Enable comparative analysis to highlight teams and players with the highest match-winning contributions.

tasks.method: Allow users to visualize clustering by team/player using heat maps to uncover match-winning performances against specific oppositions and venues.
tasks.objective: Identify contextual strengths and weaknesses for teams and players under varying conditions.
tasks.type: retrieving values | computing derived values | clustering
